results.title: Sharking
results.creator_credit: Rod Rees, Terry Walker and Educational Media Centre, RMIT
results.brief_description: A history of the shark and of shark fishing and marketing in Australia. Shows the historical development of the Australian shark industry and the detailed research and managemnet of shark fishing resources being carried out byy the Victorian Fisheries and Wildlife Department.

results.title: Sherlock Holmes faces death. Sherlock Holmes' secret weapon. Shelock Holmes and the voice of terror.
results.creator_credit: John Rawlins, Roy William Neill and Universal Pictures
results.brief_description: Three classic Holmes stories in this one compilation features Basil Rathbone as Sherlock Holmes and Nigel Bruce as Dr. Watson. Holmes, created by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle, uses his powers of observation to outwit daring criminals. In this series of films, made during World War 2, Holmes and Watson played out their adventures in a contemporary setting defeating evil Nazis, traitors and dastardly villains.
results.creator_credit_for_label: John Rawlins, Roy William Neill and Universal Pictures

results.title: Sherlock Holmes and the secret weapon [Colored version]
results.creator_credit: Roy William Neill, Howard Benedict, Hal Roach Studios and Universal Pictures
results.brief_description: Originally filmed in black & white in 1942, this colorized version was released in 1986 through Hal Roach Studios. Based on the story “The Dancing men” by Sir Arthur Conan Doyle and adapted for the screen by W. Scott Darling and Edward T. Lowe, “Sherlock Holmes and the secret weapon” is set in 1942 during WWII and has all the usual suspects expected from a Conan Doyle novella. The bomb sight (coveted secret weapon which could win the war) and its world-renowned inventor and physicist, Dr Franz Tobel (William Post, Jr) are rescued from Switzerland and the evil hands of the Nazis to the protection of the British government through a clever guise orchestrated by Sherlock Holmes (Basil Rathbone). However, on arriving in London, Holmes and the rather bumbling Dr Watson (Nigel Bruce) find out that all is not as it seems with Tobel and that London is not the safe haven as first thought, especially when the notorious Professor Moriarity (Lionel Atwill) is in league with the Nazis and will stop at nothing to get Tobel and his invention. Cast also includes Kaaren Verne, Dennis Hoey.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Roy William Neill, Howard Benedict, Hal Roach Studios and Universal Pictures

results.title: Shelter (3rd Edition)
results.creator_credit: Hermann D. Tauchert and Encyclopaedia Britannica Films
results.brief_description: Presents a look at the history of housing and community development. Illustrates the use of various types of shelters from past to present and demonstrates how a house is built.

results.title: Sharing the fruits of progress
results.creator_credit: Roy Chin, Samuel Wu, Weng Jeng-Yih and Kwang Hwa Mass Communications
results.brief_description: Reconstruction of Taiwan began after World War II. For a number of years Taiwan received foreign aid from countries like the United States. These monies were used to develop agricultural and industrial resources. In the 1980’s Taiwan was able to assist other countries with foriegn aid but as the country is not a member of key international organizations, it is restricted from doing more. This program examines Taiwan’s desire to “share the fruits of its progress.”

results.title: She's a railroader
results.creator_credit: Barbara Tranter, John Taylor, Shelah Reljic and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: Karen Zaitchik jumps on and off moving boxcars, throws switches, pulls brakes and uncouples freights with ease and confidence. She’s a railroader for CN and that’s what this 21-year-old highly individualistic woman wants out of life for the moment. This colorful short film shows how Karen manages in the traditionally male world of the railroad.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Barbara Tranter, John Taylor, Shelah Reljic and National Film Board of Canada

results.title: Shaping things: a choreographic journal
results.brief_description: Frances Alenikoff is seen working with her Frances Alenikoff Dance Theater Company. Her choreography is based on improvisation coupled with structured shape to achieve modern dance movements. She is seen discussing her ideas about dance choreography and what she hopes to achieve through her work.

results.title: Shattered dreams
results.creator_credit: Wendy Hill-Tout, Dale Phillips and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: Schizophrenia is a disease shrouded in ignorance and myth. This confronting and emotional film details the story of the Martini family who had two sons diagnosed with the disease. Clem Martini, a third brother, narrates the film and through his words we come to understand the grief and pain experienced by the schizophrenics and their families and friends. Passionate and illuminating, “Shattered Dreams” argues for an increased public knowledge of schizophrenia and mental illness.

results.title: Shark shocker; Turning turtle
results.brief_description: Two episodes of the “Blue Wilderness” series in which Ron and Valerie Taylor investigate conservation methods for sea creatures. In “Shark shocker” they go to South Africa to see an electronic shark barrier, which may deter sharks from swimming near beaches, as an alternative to nets which trap other sea creatures. In “Turning turtle” the Taylor’s show the need for rescue work with sea turtles on Raine Island off the Queensland coast.

results.title: Shantiniketan: Abode of Peace
results.creator_credit: A P Sinha, K Samarth, M. Bhavnani and Films Division, Government of India
results.brief_description: In 1901, there was established at Shantiniketan an institution for boys and girls, where education was in close communion with nature. Twenty years later, the institution had grown to such proportions that it came to be recognised as an international university, the Visva-Bharati. This film gives an interesting glimpse of Shantiniketan the “Abode of peace”, the school of international culture, established by the Poet Rabindranath Tagore at Bolpur, not far from Calcutta. Languages, sculpture, painting, embroidery, music, dancing and other fine arts are an integral part of training.
